question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNodes() in AddressBookServiceImpl] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the NodeStakeRepository to return empty results, causing getNodes() to build ConsensusNodeWrapper objects with zero stake for all nodes, making getStakeRequiredForConsensus() compute a non-zero threshold that can never be met with zero-stake nodes, permanently blocking consensus validation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNodes() in AddressBookServiceImpl] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the AddressBookRepository to return a stale address book with fewer nodes than the current network, causing the consensus threshold to be calculated against a smaller node set, potentially allowing a minority of nodes (by real network stake) to reach false consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: migrate() in AddressBookServiceImpl] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the database to be unavailable during AddressBookServiceImpl.migrate(), causing the synchronized method to throw an exception, and since getNodes() calls getCurrent() which calls migrate() on empty address book, all subsequent signature verification calls fail with InvalidDatasetException('Unable to find a valid address book')?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: health() in StreamFileHealthIndicator] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that halts stream file parsing, causing StreamFileHealthIndicator.health() to return Status.DOWN after the processing timeout window, but since the health endpoint is on a separate ActuatorHttpServer port, an external load balancer may remove the importer from rotation while the importer continues attempting (and failing) to download files, creating a split-brain scenario?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getResolvedHealthWhenNoStreamFilesParsed()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the streamCloseLatencyDurationTimer mean to be 0 (no files processed since startup), causing getResolvedHealthWhenNoStreamFilesParsed() to return the cached lastHealthStatus (UNKNOWN) indefinitely during the startup window, masking a real partition-induced outage from monitoring systems?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the streamFileNotifier.verified() call in Downloader.onVerified() to fail (e.g., Redis unavailable), throwing an uncaught exception that prevents lastStreamFile from being updated, causing the importer to re-download and re-verify the same file on every poll cycle, creating an infinite loop?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getStartAfterFilename()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the database to return a stale lastStreamFile during startup (dateRangeCalculator.getLastStreamFile()), causing the importer to re-download and re-process already-ingested stream files, potentially causing duplicate data insertion or hash chain violations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: verifyWrappedRecordBlockSignature()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes consensusNodeService.getNode() to return null for some node IDs in the SignedRecordFileProof, causing Utility.handleRecoverableError() to be called and those signatures to be skipped, potentially reducing the effective signature count below the 1/3 consensus threshold without raising a hard error?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBlockNodes() in BlockNodeDiscoveryService] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the RegisteredNodeRepository database query in discover() to throw an exception, causing discover() to return an empty list and getBlockNodes() to return only statically configured nodes, silently removing dynamically discovered block nodes from the pool without any alerting?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onRegisteredNodeChanged()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes a RegisteredNodeChangedEvent to be fired (e.g., via a transaction that updates a node), invalidating the BlockNodeDiscoveryService cache at the worst possible moment during a partition, causing getBlockNodes() to re-query the database and potentially return an empty list if the database is also partitioned?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: tryReadmit()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to time attacks against BlockNode.tryReadmit(), where the attacker repeatedly causes connection failures to exhaust maxSubscribeAttempts, then waits for readmitTime to expire and immediately causes another failure, keeping a block node permanently cycling between active and inactive states and preventing stable block streaming?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: drainGrpcBuffer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes a gRPC stream to be cancelled mid-transfer, causing BlockNodeSubscriber.drainGrpcBuffer() to submit a task to the single-threaded executor that blocks indefinitely reading from a hung gRPC call, exhausting the executor and preventing future buffer drain tasks from running, leading to memory leaks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBlockNodes() in BlockNodeSubscriber] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the block node properties to change (e.g., discovery returns different nodes), triggering nodesChanged() to return true, causing all existing BlockNode connections to be closed and new ones created, and if the new connections immediately fail due to the partition, all block nodes are in error state simultaneously?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the DEMO network check in downloadNextBatch() to trigger (sigFilesMap.isEmpty() && DEMO network), setting downloaderProperties.setEnabled(false) permanently, and if the network property is misconfigured or spoofed to appear as DEMO during a partition, the downloader is permanently disabled without any recovery mechanism?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: setStreamFileIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the importer to restart with a corrupted lastStreamFile state, causing setStreamFileIndex() to compute an incorrect index (lastStreamFile.get().map(StreamFile::getIndex).map(v -> v + 1)) that skips blocks, creating gaps in the indexed stream file sequence that downstream consumers cannot detect?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onBlockStream() in AbstractBlockSource] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes blockStreamReader.read() to receive a partial/truncated block stream, throwing an exception that propagates through onBlockStream() and is caught by CompositeBlockSource.get()'s catch(Throwable t) block, incrementing the error counter and potentially switching sources, while the partial block data is silently discarded?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onEndOfBlock() in BlockAssembler] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes a block node to send an EndOfBlock message with a mismatched block number (blockHeader.getNumber() != blockNumber), causing Utility.handleRecoverableError() to be called (non-fatal), and the block is still assembled and processed with the mismatched metadata, potentially corrupting block sequence tracking?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: append() in BlockAssembler] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes a block node to send block items exceeding streamProperties.getMaxBlockItems(), causing BlockStreamException('Too many block items in a pending block') to be thrown, which triggers onError() in BlockNode, and if this happens repeatedly across all nodes, all nodes are marked inactive simultaneously?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: timeout() in BlockAssembler] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes a block node to send the first BlockItemSet (starting the stopwatch) but then stall, causing BlockAssembler.timeout() to return a negative value (timeout.toMillis() - stopwatch.elapsed() < 0), which is passed to grpcCall.read() as a negative timeout, potentially causing undefined behavior in the gRPC blocking call?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: verifySignature() in NodeSignatureVerifier] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the address book to be unavailable, resulting in node.getPublicKey() returning null for all nodes, causing verifySignature() to return false for every signature, and since no signatures reach VERIFIED status, consensusValidator.validate() throws SignatureVerificationException, halting all ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: verify() in Downloader] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the importer to restart between processing two consecutive stream files, resetting lastStreamFile to Optional.empty(), causing verifyHashChain() to skip chain verification (SHA_384.isHashEmpty returns true for null), allowing a stream file with an incorrect previousHash to be accepted as valid?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: statusMap()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that makes all nodes unreachable, causing statusMap() to populate the NOT_FOUND set with all node IDs and increment their counters via nodeSignatureStatusMetricMap, creating unbounded metric cardinality growth in the ConcurrentHashMap as new Counter objects are created for each node/status combination during extended partitions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNextFirstStageActiveStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition during the cutover first stage that causes both blockStreamAdvanced to be false and startWrappedRecordBlockConsensusTimestamp to be non-null, causing the system to evaluate wrappedRecordBlockStopwatch elapsed time against maxLatency, and if the partition lasts longer than maxLatency, the system falls back to RECORD stream which is also partitioned, creating an oscillation loop?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: shouldTryFirstStage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that prevents the HAPI version from being updated in the last record file, causing shouldTryFirstStage() to always return false (version check fails), permanently bypassing the first-stage cutover logic and forcing the system into single-stage cutover mode where it oscillates every threshold seconds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: get() in CutoverServiceImpl] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the synchronized CutoverServiceImpl.get() method to block indefinitely (e.g., task.run() hangs due to network I/O), preventing any other thread from acquiring the monitor lock and effectively deadlocking the entire cutover coordination mechanism?
